description: Ground-truth annotations for APK test fixtures. Documents exact security findings each fixture should and should NOT trigger, keyed by filename. Used for regression testing, false-positive tracking, and MobSF baseline comparison.

MANIFEST-001.title: Application is debuggable
MANIFEST-001.description: android:debuggable=true allows debugger attachment, memory inspection, runtime modification

MANIFEST-002.title: Application allows backup
MANIFEST-002.description: android:allowBackup=true (or default for targetSdk<31) allows adb backup extraction

MANIFEST-003.title: Permits cleartext HTTP traffic
MANIFEST-003.description: android:usesCleartextTraffic=true or default true for targetSdk<28

MANIFEST-005.title: Outdated minSdkVersion
MANIFEST-005.description: minSdk<19 = high (no TLS 1.2), 19-23 = low (no NSC enforcement), >=24 = no finding

MANIFEST-008.title: StrandHogg 1.0 task hijacking
MANIFEST-008.description: Non-default taskAffinity + allowTaskReparenting enables task hijacking

MANIFEST-009.title: StrandHogg 2.0 task hijacking
MANIFEST-009.description: Exported singleTask/singleInstance activity; high if minSdk<29, low if >=29

MANIFEST-010.title: Browsable intents / App Links
MANIFEST-010.description: Custom schemes = low, HTTP/HTTPS without autoVerify = medium

MANIFEST-011.title: Network Security Config issues
MANIFEST-011.description: Cleartext permitted (global=high, domain=medium), user CAs trusted (high), debug overrides (info)

MANIFEST-013.title: Implicit intent hijacking
MANIFEST-013.description: Exported services/receivers with intent-filters but no permission protection

MANIFEST-014.title: Signing scheme version
MANIFEST-014.description: Not signed = critical, v1-only = medium, lacks v3 = info

MANIFEST-015.title: Custom backup agent
MANIFEST-015.description: backupAgent with allowBackup=true = medium, with allowBackup=false = info

MANIFEST-016.title: High-risk dangerous permissions
MANIFEST-016.description: Grouped by category; SMS/Phone/Surveillance = high, Location/PII/Storage = medium

MANIFEST-017.title: Intent scheme hijacking (data URIs)
MANIFEST-017.description: Intent filters accepting file://, content://, javascript://, intent:// schemes

MANIFEST-018.title: Shared User ID
MANIFEST-018.description: android:sharedUserId set; system UIDs = info, custom UIDs = medium

debuggable.apk.description: APK with android:debuggable=true; all other attributes are secure
debuggable.apk.notes: allowBackup=false, usesCleartextTraffic=false, minSdk=28, targetSdk=33 — all safe

allow_backup.apk.notes: No backupAgent set, so MANIFEST-015 should not trigger

MANIFEST-004.notes: Test-only APKs should never ship in production

test_only.apk.notes: All other attributes set to safe values

min_sdk_outdated.apk.description: APK with critically outdated minSdkVersion=15 (< API 19, no TLS 1.2)
min_sdk_outdated.apk.notes: targetSdk=33 is modern so cleartext defaults are safe; allowBackup=false explicit

exported_components.apk.description: APK with 8 exported components (5 activities, 1 service, 1 receiver, 1 provider) without permission protection

MANIFEST-006.notes: 8 unprotected exports >= 5 threshold = high severity. Launcher activity excluded from count.

exported_components.apk.notes: Components are explicitly exported=true (targetSdk>=31 requires explicit export)

weak_permissions.apk.description: APK with 2 custom permissions using weak protectionLevel (normal + dangerous)
weak_permissions.apk.notes: Custom perms are declared but not used to protect any component

strandhogg_v1.apk.description: APK with StrandHogg v1 task hijacking pattern: non-default taskAffinity + allowTaskReparenting

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-006: 2 exported activities (HijackActivity, TaskHijack2) may trigger low/medium export finding
may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-009: TaskHijack2 has singleTask launch mode and is exported

strandhogg_v1.apk.notes: Primary check is MANIFEST-008; secondary findings from exports and task reparenting are expected side effects

strandhogg_v2.apk.description: APK with StrandHogg v2 pattern: exported singleTask and singleInstance activities

MANIFEST-009.notes: minSdk=26 < 29 threshold = high severity. Two vulnerable activities: VulnActivity1 (singleTask), VulnActivity2 (singleInstance)

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-005: minSdk=26 is >= 24 threshold so should NOT trigger, but verify
may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-006: 2 exported activities may trigger low export finding

strandhogg_v2.apk.notes: Primary check is MANIFEST-009; minSdk=26 is above the 24 threshold so MANIFEST-005 should not fire

app_links.apk.description: APK with browsable intents: custom scheme (myapp://) and HTTPS without autoVerify

sub_findings.description: Custom scheme deep link (myapp://open) - no verification possible

MANIFEST-010.notes: Two sub-findings: custom scheme = low, unverified HTTPS = medium

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-006: 2 exported activities (DeepLinkActivity, WebLinkActivity) may trigger low export finding

app_links.apk.notes: MANIFEST-017 should NOT trigger because schemes are myapp:// and https://, not intent:// or file://

network_security_config.apk.description: APK with insecure network security config: global cleartext, user CA trust, debug overrides, wildcard domain config

sub_findings.description: Global cleartext traffic permitted in base-config
User-installed CA certificates trusted in base-config

MANIFEST-011.notes: Multiple NSC sub-findings; highest severity (high) dominates

network_security_config.apk.notes: usesCleartextTraffic=false in manifest, but NSC overrides it; MANIFEST-003 depends on manifest attribute only

task_reparenting.apk.description: APK with android:allowTaskReparenting=true on application element

MANIFEST-012.notes: Application-level task reparenting allows activity stack manipulation

task_reparenting.apk.notes: MANIFEST-008 requires non-default taskAffinity in addition to reparenting; this only has reparenting

implicit_intent.apk.description: APK with exported service and receiver using intent-filters without permission protection

MANIFEST-013.notes: VulnService and VulnReceiver are exported with intent-filters but no permission

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-006: 2 exported components (VulnService, VulnReceiver) may trigger low export finding

implicit_intent.apk.notes: MANIFEST-013 specifically targets services/receivers (not activities) with implicit intents

signing_scheme.apk.description: APK signed with v1 only (JAR signing), missing v2/v3

MANIFEST-014.notes: V1-only signing is vulnerable to zipfile modification attacks

signing_scheme.apk.notes: Signing check inspects APK signature metadata, not manifest XML

backup_agent.apk.description: APK with custom backupAgent and allowBackup=true

MANIFEST-015.notes: Custom backupAgent + allowBackup=true = medium severity (may not encrypt backup data)

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-002: allowBackup=true is present, so MANIFEST-002 will also fire

backup_agent.apk.notes: MANIFEST-002 is expected as a side effect since allowBackup=true is required for the backup agent risk

dangerous_permissions.apk.description: APK requesting 8 dangerous permissions across SMS, surveillance, location, and PII categories

MANIFEST-016.notes: SMS and surveillance categories are high-risk = overall high severity

dangerous_permissions.apk.notes: All 8 permissions are grouped by category; having high-risk categories (SMS, surveillance) makes overall severity high

intent_scheme.apk.description: APK with browsable activity handling intent:// scheme URLs

MANIFEST-017.notes: intent:// scheme on exported browsable activity enables arbitrary intent injection

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-006: SchemeHandlerActivity is exported, may trigger low export finding
may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-010: Activity has BROWSABLE category, may also trigger app link finding

intent_scheme.apk.notes: Primary concern is intent:// scheme injection (CWE-94), not generic deep link hijacking

shared_user_id.apk.description: APK with android:sharedUserId set to custom UID

MANIFEST-018.notes: Custom sharedUserId (com.test.shared) = medium severity; deprecated since API 29

shared_user_id.apk.notes: System UIDs like android.uid.system would produce info severity instead

kitchen_sink.apk.description: APK with maximum vulnerability surface: triggers 15+ manifest checks simultaneously

MANIFEST-006.notes: 10+ exported components without permission (5 activities + StrandHogg activities + service + provider + DeepLinkActivity + IntentSchemeActivity)

MANIFEST-009.notes: StrandHogg2Activity has singleTask launch mode + exported + minSdk=15 < 29

MANIFEST-016.notes: SMS, surveillance, location, PII, phone permissions

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-011: Has network_security_config with cleartext permitted and user CAs trusted

kitchen_sink.apk.notes: Kitchen sink intentionally triggers maximum checks. MANIFEST-011/012/014 may also fire depending on NSC parsing and signing mock.

clean.apk.description: Clean APK with zero manifest vulnerabilities — the negative control
clean.apk.notes: minSdk=28 (modern), targetSdk=34, allowBackup=false, usesCleartextTraffic=false, no exports, no custom permissions, no dangerous permissions (only INTERNET)

secure_full.apk.description: Fully hardened APK: modern SDK, all security attributes explicitly set to safe values, strict NSC, v2+v3 signing
secure_full.apk.notes: Gold standard for secure configuration. NSC has cleartext=false and system CAs only. Non-exported service and receiver present but safe.

secure_with_exports.apk.description: APK with exported components properly protected by signature-level permissions
secure_with_exports.apk.notes: Exported provider and service are protected by com.test.secure.exports.PROVIDER_ACCESS (signature level). MANIFEST-006 should NOT fire because permission is set. MANIFEST-007 should NOT fire because protectionLevel=signature.

secure_custom_perms.apk.description: APK with custom permissions at signature/signatureOrSystem level; single CAMERA permission is below threshold
secure_custom_perms.apk.notes: Custom permissions use signature/signatureOrSystem (not weak). CAMERA is only 1 dangerous permission — below MANIFEST-016 multi-category threshold.

secure_network_config.apk.description: APK with proper network_security_config: cleartext disabled, system CAs only, certificate pinning with 2 pins and valid expiration
secure_network_config.apk.notes: NSC is properly configured: cleartext=false, system CAs only, pin-set with 2 pins and future expiration. Should NOT trigger MANIFEST-011.

secure_minimal.apk.description: Minimal secure APK relying on safe Android defaults for high SDK levels (API 30+)
secure_minimal.apk.notes: minSdk=30, targetSdk=34. allowBackup=false and usesCleartextTraffic=false are explicitly set. v2+v3 signing.

secure_complex.apk.description: Complex app with multiple component types (activities, service, receiver, provider) — all non-exported or properly secured
secure_complex.apk.notes: All components exported=false. Standard permissions only (INTERNET, ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE, FOREGROUND_SERVICE, RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED, VIBRATE). No dangerous permissions.

diva.apk.description: DIVA (Damn Insecure and Vulnerable App) v1.0 — intentionally vulnerable Android app for security training

MANIFEST-003.notes: Default-based: targetSdk=24 < 28 so cleartext defaults to permitted. Confidence is medium because it is inferred, not explicit.

MANIFEST-005.notes: minSdk=15 (< 19 threshold = critically outdated, no TLS 1.2 support)

MANIFEST-006.notes: 13 activities with intent-filters, targetSdk=24 < 31 so implicit export applies. ~10+ are unprotected after excluding launcher. Confidence is medium due to implicit export inference.

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-014: Signing scheme depends on actual APK binary signing method
may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-016: Depends on DIVA's actual permission list; may have some dangerous permissions

mobsf_baseline.notes: MobSF reports 5-6 core manifest findings for DIVA; our scanner should match within tolerance

diva.apk.notes: Real-world APK. Confidence may vary for default-inferred findings. MANIFEST-003 is default-based (not explicit).

insecurebankv2.apk.description: InsecureBankv2 — intentionally vulnerable banking app for security testing

MANIFEST-003.notes: Default-based: targetSdk=15 < 28 so cleartext defaults to permitted

MANIFEST-006.notes: Multiple exported activities via implicit export (targetSdk=15 < 31). ~8 unprotected components.

MANIFEST-010.notes: ViewStatement activity has BROWSABLE intent-filter with http scheme

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-014: Signing scheme depends on actual APK binary

mobsf_baseline.notes: MobSF baseline for InsecureBankv2 is approximately 6-8 manifest findings

firmware_context_test.notes: When placed in /system/priv-app/, all severities bump +1

insecurebankv2.apk.notes: Real-world APK. Very old targetSdk (15) means many default-based findings.

ovaa.apk.description: OVAA (Oversecured Vulnerable Android App) — intentionally vulnerable app showcasing real Android vulnerabilities

MANIFEST-005.notes: minSdk=23 is below the secure threshold of 24 but >= 19, so low severity (not critically outdated)

MANIFEST-006.notes: 11+ exported components including activities, providers, and receiver. High severity (>= 5 unprotected).

MANIFEST-009.notes: TheftActivity has singleTask launch mode + exported + minSdk=23 < 29 = high severity

MANIFEST-010.notes: Two deep link findings: custom scheme (low) and unverified HTTPS (medium)

may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-013: Exported receiver with intent-filter may trigger implicit intent check
may_also_trigger.MANIFEST-016: Depends on OVAA's actual permission list

mobsf_baseline.notes: MobSF reports approximately 6-8 manifest findings for OVAA

ovaa.apk.notes: Real-world APK. MANIFEST-001 should NOT trigger (OVAA release build is not debuggable). MANIFEST-005 is low severity (23 >= 19 but < 24).

firmware_context_modifiers.description: How firmware context affects severity. Applied on top of base findings.

priv_app.notes: Privileged apps have elevated system access; vulnerabilities are more impactful

platform_signed.rule: MANIFEST-001 escalates to critical. Eligible checks get -1 severity reduction (net 0 with priv-app bump).
platform_signed.applies_when: APK is signed with platform certificate
platform_signed.notes: Platform-signed system components operate under different threat model

debug_signed.applies_when: APK is signed with debug certificate
debug_signed.notes: Debug builds are expected during development but should not ship

test_matrix_summary.negative_test_coverage: Each secure-baseline fixture asserts ALL 18 checks must NOT trigger
